danger_signals.visual_form: Player arrangement on field; defensive line positioning
danger_signals.meaning: Indicates threat level and play type intention

danger_signals.visual_form: Ball location relative to end zones
danger_signals.meaning: Red zone proximity indicates scoring threat or defensive vulnerability
Sudden shift from offensive opportunity to defensive vulnerability

safety_signals.visual_form: Ball control indicator; player control highlighting
Yard markers; visual feedback on progress

safety_signals.meaning: Reduced scoring threat; increased defensive security

faction_indicators.color_identity: Primary uniform color of selected NFL team
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Official NFL team logo and uniform design
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Opponent team colors and logo; contrasting uniform palette
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Team-colored field markers; sideline personnel in team apparel

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Official NFL team logo of defensive team
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Distinct from offensive team through uniform differentiation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Field paused; play-call mode interface active; players stationary in formation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: No player movement; awaiting user input for play execution

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Animated player movement; ball in flight or carrier motion; field dynamic
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: AI-controlled defensive positioning; player route execution; real-time play development

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Possession indicator change; field perspective shift or rotation preparation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Control transfer between player and AI; formation reset; momentum reversal

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Field rotates; screen pauses to reorient perspective to opposite team's viewpoint
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Camera rotation instead of instant reversal; prevents player disorientation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Field rotatable to any angle; play viewable from multiple camera positions
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player-controlled camera; temporal pause; analytical observation mode

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Special team uniforms available; championship-tier roster display
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Expanded team selection without special codes; elevated competition state

environmental_storytelling.example: Authentic 1993-1994 season team logos and uniforms communicate legitimacy and real-world sports context; players recognize actual franchises
Visual progression indicators show distance to goal; communicate strategic geography
Team-colored sideline staff and coaching positions reinforce faction identity and organizational structure
Defensive and offensive line positioning communicates tactical approach and strategic intent
Color contrast between opposing teams provides immediate faction identification without text overlay

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Stationary in formation; awaiting snap signal
Static alignment; positioned for coverage/run defense assignments
Execute assigned routes; move toward receivers or blocking assignments
Pursue ball carrier or cover assigned receivers; react to play development
Move downfield; attempt to advance toward opposing end zone
Transition from reactive to aggressive offensive posture

notes: Madden NFL '94 represents a pivotal moment in sports game visual communication through introduction of official NFL licensing. The game's manifestation layer communicates world-state primarily through formation positioning, field perspective, and uniform differentiation rather than explicit textual indicators. The play-call and pause-rotate systems for special teams plays address a critical usability issue (disorientation) through thoughtful visual design. No explicit danger/safety signals exist beyond game-state positional indicators; threat communication relies on player reading of formation tactics rather than visual warnings.
